evals.prompt: How do I write table-driven tests in Go?
evals.expected_output: Define a slice of test cases with name, input, expected fields. Use t.Run() for subtests. Assert with testify. Cover nil, empty, and error cases.

assertions.description: Mentions table or slice of test cases

evals.prompt: How do I generate mocks for Go interfaces?
evals.expected_output: Use mockery to auto-generate mocks from interfaces. Do not write manual mocks. Define mocks locally in test scope to avoid state leakage.

evals.prompt: How do I detect race conditions in Go tests?
evals.expected_output: Run go test -race. The race detector instruments all memory accesses and reports data races. Should be part of CI pipeline.

should_not_trigger: How do I write NestJS unit tests?
How do I test a React component?
How do I configure a Go HTTP server?
